scenario: Harbor Bank operates 26000 transactions per day in Camunda 8.8 and a process instance is stuck after a job worker has failed with no retries left. The operations team needs to confirm what an incident represents in Camunda 8 and which of the listed situations creates one for this kind of failure mode.
question: Which statement correctly describes what an incident is in Camunda 8 and which situation creates one in this case?

options.text: An incident is a successful retry event raised when a job worker reactivates a failed job within its retry budget without any operator interaction needed
An incident is a problem in process execution that leaves the instance stuck and requires user interaction, created when a failed job has no retries left
An incident is a Zeebe log record written for every error the broker observes, including transient network errors that recover automatically without intervention
An incident is a BPMN history attribute exported to Operate after the process instance completes, recording every caught error event along the way

a.text: Incorrect. A successful retry is the opposite of an incident; the docs define an incident as a problem in process execution that requires user interaction, not a self-healing retry event.

b.text: Correct. The incidents concept page defines an incident as a problem in process execution that leaves the instance stuck and lists a job that has failed with no retries left as one of the situations that create one.

c.text: Incorrect. The docs explicitly note that not all errors lead to incidents; unexpected errors in Zeebe do not always result in incidents, and transient errors are not always represented.

d.text: Incorrect. Incidents are runtime indicators of stuck process instances, not historical attributes set after completion; they are also distinct from caught error events, which do not create incidents.

explanation: The incidents concept page defines an incident as a problem in process execution that leaves a process instance stuck and requires user interaction. The listed creation triggers include a failed job with no retries left, a non-boolean condition, a timer expression with an unexpected type, a decision that cannot be evaluated and an uncaught BPMN error.
